title: Build Qwen3.5 from Scratch
description: Hybrid attention, Mixture-of-Experts, and local deployment — build the architecture that lets a 9B model outperform one 13x its size.

notebooks.title: From Softmax to Linear Attention
notebooks.objective: Understand the O(N²) bottleneck of standard attention and derive the linear attention reformulation from first principles.

notebooks.title: DeltaNet: Error-Correcting Memory
notebooks.objective: Build the delta rule update and compare naive linear attention vs DeltaNet on a retrieval task.

notebooks.title: Gated DeltaNet: The Full Layer
notebooks.objective: Implement the complete Gated DeltaNet layer with Conv1D, SiLU, L2 norm, exponential gating, and delta rule updates.

notebooks.title: Hybrid Attention + MoE
notebooks.objective: Assemble the 3:1 hybrid attention pattern and implement fine-grained MoE with top-k routing and a shared expert.

notebooks.title: Build & Run Mini-Qwen3.5 Locally
notebooks.objective: Build a mini Qwen3.5 from scratch, train it, load official weights, quantize, and run inference locally.

caseStudy.title: Building a Local AI Coding Assistant for a Privacy-Conscious Healthcare Startup
caseStudy.subtitle: MedCode Health — HIPAA-Compliant On-Device Code Intelligence
caseStudy.description: MedCode Health deploys Qwen3.5-9B locally using llama.cpp to build a HIPAA-compliant coding assistant for their medical records system, achieving $0 API costs and sub-50ms latency.

curator.name: Dr. Rajat Dandekar
curator.title: Course Instructor
curator.bio: Dr. Rajat Dandekar is a researcher and educator specializing in AI/ML, with a passion for making complex concepts accessible through intuitive explanations and hands-on learning.
